title: EFT: Unfinished Business
description: Three practical clinician-facing slides extracted from the EFT interventions practice deck.

sources.label: Vrana and Greenberg, Overview of Emotion-Focused Therapy

slides.title: Marker: unresolved feeling toward a significant other
slides.body: Use when the absent other is emotionally present in the room.
bullets: Listen for lingering hurt, anger, longing, grief, or blocked protest.
Clarify who belongs in the chair and what scene is alive now.
Do not force forgiveness; track completion, not moral resolution.

slides.title: Empty-chair arc
slides.body: Move from global complaint to specific emotional contact, then toward a new internal stance.
steps: Bring the other into the chair vividly enough to evoke contact.
Express primary emotion and unmet need directly to the other.
If useful, enact the other's response and test its impact.
Return to the self: what is true, what was missing, what is no longer owed.
Consolidate a stance: accountability, boundary, grief, release, or compassion.

slides.title: Do not mistake these for resolution
slides.body: The task can look dramatic while staying emotionally unchanged. Track organismic shift.
items: Insight without contact: 'I understand why they did it.'
Premature forgiveness that bypasses anger or grief.
Retaliatory expression that leaves the vulnerable need untouched.
A polished narrative with no bodily relief or new boundary.
